slides.title:
The most dangerous
document is the one
that's *confidently wrong*

Diagrams show
*design day*
slides.body: Production diverged the week it went live.

slides.title: The drift is invisible
slides.body:
The 11pm emergency change.
The "temporary" VLAN, three years old.
The failover rewired mid-outage — never redrawn.

slides.title:
Wrong docs beat no docs?
*Backwards.*
slides.body:
No docs: you verify everything. Slow, but honest.
Wrong docs: you trust.

slides.title: 2am, mid-outage
slides.body:
You walk the "documented" failover path
while the real one *burns*.

slides.title:
The fix isn't
drawing discipline
slides.body:
Generate docs *from* the network, not about it.
Configs are evidence. Diagrams are memories.

slides.title: Where truth lives
steps: *Truth lives only in the running network*

slides.title:
Document
the truth
slides.body:
What did a "current" diagram cost you at 2am?
Follow *@mzsnetworks* for operational reality.
